title: Influenza A and B virus WGS using Illumina Microbial Amplicon Prep (iMAP) half reaction
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"d6nm9","text":"This protocol describes whole-genome sequencing (WGS) of Influenza A and B viruses using the Illumina Microbial Amplicon Prep (iMAP). The protocol has been adapted by miniaturising reagent use to half-reactions and by incorporating primers originally developed by Zhou and Wentworth (2012) and (2014) for effective genome amplification of Influenza viruses. In addition, the standard pooled library clean-up procedure was modified to perform individual library clean-ups, which improved overall library quality and consistency before sequencing.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"54tcb","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"cfueb","text":"This protocol starts with extracted RNA or TNA.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8tp15","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ev8pa","text":"We use pre-sequencing sample qualification by RT-qPCR:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1mc3t","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dc5nm","text":"RNA/TNA extracts are screened using the TrueMark SARS-CoV-2 Flu A Flu B RSV Select Panel. ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":40,"length":48}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e4s8l","text":"Samples with Ct values above 30 are excluded from sequencing. ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bdjl8","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5bc8d","text":"As quality controls, we include a positive control, consisting of a commercially prepared strain or a previously sequenced sample, and a negative control (nuclease-free water) in every run.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"68fp4","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"alfhk","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Prepare the Influenza AB primer pool as described here

5.1 Bind amplicons to beads 1) Centrifuge the PCR plate at 500 x g for 1 minute. 2) Vortex to resuspend the IPB and add 9 µL to each well of the PCR plate. 3) Vortex to mix and centrifuge briefly. 4) Incubate at room temperature for 5 minutes._ 5) Place it on a magnetic stand and wait for the liquid to clear (~5 minutes). 6) Remove and discard the supernatant. 5.2 Ethanol wash 7) Wash beads as follows: a. Keep the plate on the magnetic stand and add 100 µL 80% EtOH to each well. b. Wait 30 seconds, then remove and discard the supernatant. 8) Wash the beads a second time._ 9) Centrifuge the plate briefly to collect any remaining EtOH at the bottom. 10) Place it on the magnetic stand and wait for the liquid to clear. 5.3 Dry and elute 11) Use a P20 pipette to remove ALL residual EtOH**. 12) Allow to air-dry on the magnetic stand for 1 minute._ 13) Add 16 µL RSB to resuspend the beads. 14) Vortex to mix and then centrifuge briefly. 15) Incubate at room temperature for 2 minutes._ 16) Place the plate on the magnetic rack and wait for the liquid to clear (~3 minutes). 17) Transfer 15 µL of supernatant to the wells of a new PCR plate labelled cDNA**. ✓ At least 10% of the samples should be checked post-PCR using a Qubit Fluorometer and Qubit DNA HS Assay to ensure amplification was successful.

4.1 Prepare Tagmentation Master Mix In a microcentrifuge tube, combine the following reagents to prepare the Tagmentation Master Mix**: Reagent volumes include overage Reagent Volume (μL) n=1 1 EBLT 2 2 TB1 6 3 NFW 10 4 Total 18 1) Add 15 µL of the tagmentation master mix to each well of a new PCR plate labelled TAG**. 4.2 Add cDNA and run tagmentation 2) Add 10 µL from the cDNA plate to the corresponding wells of the TAG plate. 3) Seal, vortex (1600 rpm for 1 min), centrifuge (500 x g for 1 min). 4) Place on a thermal cycler and run the iMAP_TAG program. _Preheat Lid: 105 °C and Reaction Volume: 25 µL_ iMAP_TAG Program Temperature (°C) Time 1 55 5 minutes 2 10 Hold

6.1 Bind and neutralize 1) After the TAG program, immediately add 5 µL ST2 to each well of the TAG plate. 2) Seal, vortex (1600 rpm for 1 min), centrifuge (500 x g for 1 min). 3) Incubate at room temperature for 5 minutes._ 4) Centrifuge briefly. 5) Place it on a magnetic stand and wait until the liquid is clear (~3 minutes). 6) Remove and discard the supernatant. 6.2 TWB washes 7) Wash beads as follows: 8) Remove the plate from the magnetic stand. 9) Add 50 µL TWB to each well. 10) Seal, vortex (1600 rpm for 1 min), centrifuge (500 x g for 1 min). 11) Place it on the magnetic stand and wait until the liquid is clear (~2 minutes) 12) Remove and discard the supernatant. 13) Wash the beads a second time (leave supernatant in the plate after the second wash to prevent beads from overdrying).

In a microcentrifuge tube, combine the following reagents to prepare the TAG PCR Master Mix**: 1) Vortex the TAG PCR master mix. 2) Keep the TAG plate on the magnetic stand and remove the TWB. 3) Use a P20 pipette to remove any remaining TWB**. 4) Remove the TAG plate from the magnetic stand. 5) Add 20 µL TAG PCR master mix to each well. 6) Add 5 µL Index Adapters to each well. 7) Seal, vortex (1600 rpm for 1 min), centrifuge (500 x g for 1 min). 8) Place on a thermal cycler and run the iMAP_TAG PCR program. _Preheat Lid: 100 °C and Reaction Volume: 25 µL_ | iMAP_TAG PCR PROGRAM | | |--------------------------|-------------| | Temperature (°C) | Time | | 72 | 3 minutes | | 98 | 3 minutes | | 98 | 20 seconds | | 60 | 30 seconds | 7 cycles* | | 72 | 1 minutes | | 72 | 3 minutes | | 10 | Hold | *To compensate for low cDNA input amounts, the number of PCR cycles can be increased as follows: | Total cDNA Input (ng) | Number of PCR cycles | |-----------------------|----------------------| | 1-24 | 12 | | 25-99 | 9 | | 100-500 | 7 | _Safe Stopping Point – seal the plate and store at -25°C to -15°C

1) Centrifuge the TAG plate at 500 x g, °C, for 00:01:00 . 2) Place it on a magnetic stand and wait until the liquid is clear 00:03:00 . 3) Label a new PCR plate IPB. 4) Vortex the Illumina Purification Beads Illumina Illumina Purification Beads Illumina tube to resuspend and add 22.5 µL to each well of the IPB plate. 5) Transfer 25 µL supernatant from each well of the TAG plate to the corresponding wells of the IPB plate. 6) Vortex to mix and centrifuge briefly. 7) Incubate at °C for 00:05:00 ._ 8) Place it on a magnetic stand and wait for the liquid to clear ( 00:05:00 ). 9) Remove and discard the supernatant. 10) Wash beads as follows: a. Keep the plate on the magnetic stand and add 100 µL 80% EtOH to each well. b. Wait 00:00:30 , then remove and discard the supernatant. 11) Wash the beads a second time. 12) Centrifuge the plate briefly to collect any remaining EtOH at the bottom. 13) Place it on the magnetic stand and wait for the liquid to clear. 14) Use a P20 pipette to remove ALL residual EtOH. 15) Allow to air-dry on the magnetic stand for 00:01:00 . 16) Add 16 µL Resuspension Buffer Illumina to resuspend the beads. 17) Vortex to mix and then centrifuge briefly. 18) Incubate at room temperature for 00:02:00 . 19) Place the plate on the magnetic rack and wait for the liquid to clear ( 00:03:00 ). 20) Transfer 15 µL of supernatant to a new PCR plate labelled LIB. Safe Stopping Point - seal the plate and store at -25°C to -15°C

Analyze 1 µL of each prepared library using Qubit™ dsDNA HS Assay Kit Invitrogen - Thermo Fisher . Using the quantifications for each library, pool libraries equally by mass (ng) in a clean 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube . Vortex to mix and centrifuge briefly. Analyze 1 µL of the library pool using the Qubit™ dsDNA HS Assay Kit Invitrogen - Thermo Fisher . (If the concentration is too high, dilute it down to a suitable concentration for TapeStation and qPCR) Run 1 µL of the library pool on a using the D1000 Screen Tape Agilent Technologies . The library pool is then quantified by qPCR using the Library Quantification Kit Takara Bio Catalog #Cat. No. 638319 . The molarity of the library pool is calculated using the average fragment size obtained from the TapeStation (expected ~350 bp). Using the quantification obtained from qPCR, the library pool is then normalized to a starting concentration ( 2-4 nanomolar (nM) ) using Resuspension Buffer Illumina . Follow the denature and dilute instructions for your chosen system to dilute to the final loading concentration. Sequencing Platform Starting Concentration (nM) Final Loading Concentration (pM) 1 MiSeq V2 4 10 2 MiSeq V3 4 12 3 NextSeq 2000 (Standard) 2 750 4 NextSeq 2000 (XLEAP) 2 650 5 MiSeq i100 2 80 Recommended Loading Concentrations by Platform

Individual primers were obtained as desalted, dried stock from Integrated DNA Technologies (IDT). Each primer must be resuspended in 0.1× TE buffer to a final concentration of 100 micromolar (µM) , as per the individual specification sheets supplied.

Add the volumes specified in the table below for each primer to the primer pool. It is recommended to make more than one pool to prevent multiple freeze-thaw cycles. Primer Name Primer sequence 5' to 3' Stock Conc. (µM) Volume (µL) into pool 1 Uni12/Inf-1 GGGGGGAGCAAAAGCAGG 100 16.8 2 Uni12/Inf-3 GGGGGGAGCGAAAGCAGG 100 25.2 3 Uni13/Inf-1 CGGGTTATTAGTAGAAACAAGG 100 42 4 B-PBs-UniF GGGGGGAGCAGAAGCGGAGC 100 12 5 B-PBs-UniR CCGGGTTATTAGTAGAAACACGAGC 100 12 6 B-PA-UniF GGGGGGAGCAGAAGCGGTGC 100 5 7 B-PA-UniR CCGGGTTATTAGTAGAAACACGTGC 100 5 8 B-HANA-UniF GGGGGGAGCAGAAGCAGAGC 100 10 9 B-HANA-UniR CCGGGTTATTAGTAGTAACAAGAGC 100 10 10 B-NP-UniF GGGGGGAGCAGAAGCACAGC 100 8 11 B-NP-UniR CCGGGTTATTAGTAGAAACAACAGC 100 8 12 B-M-Uni3F GGGGGGAGCAGAAGCACGCACTT 100 4 13 B-Mg-Uni3F GGGGGGAGCAGAAGCAGGCACTT 100 4 14 B-M-Uni3R CCGGGTTATTAGTAGAAACAACGCACTT 100 8 15 B-NS-Uni3F GGGGGGAGCAGAAGCAGAGGATT 100 6 16 B-NS-Uni3R CCGGGTTATTAGTAGTAACAAGAGGATT 100 6 Influenza A virus and Influenza B virus primers Alternatively, the Illumina Microbial Amplicon Prep - Influenza A/B Kit ( Catalogue #20106305) can be purchased, which includes the influenza A/B targeting primer pool.
Each pool is then diluted to 10 micromolar (µM) working stocks using nuclease-free water. The 10 micromolar (µM) stock dilutions are to be used for the One-Step RT-PCR.

Prepare the one-step RT-PCR Master mix and perform PCR following the steps below. Reagent Storage Instructions 1 FSM (First Strand Mix) -25°C to -15°C Thaw at room temperature. Vortex to mix and keep on ice. 2 RVT (Reverse Transcriptase) -25°C to -15°C Thaw on ice. Vortex to mix and keep on ice. 3 Flu AB Primer Pool -25°C to -15°C Thaw on ice. Vortex to mix and keep on ice. 4 IPM (Illumina PCR Mix) -25°C to -15°C Thaw on ice. Vortex to mix and keep on ice. Overview of reagents

In a microcentrifuge tube, combine the following reagents to prepare the One-Step RT-PCR Master Mix. The table indicates the required volume for 1 sample. Calculate the volumes required for the amount of samples included in the run. Reagent Volume (μL) per sample Required volume for x samples 1 FSM 1.6 ... 2 RVT 0.5 ... 3 IPM 7.5 ... 4 Primer Pool (10 µM) 0.6 ... 5 Nuclease-free water 1.8 ... 6 Total 12.0 ... RT-PCR Master Mix
Add 12 µL master mix to each well of a new 96 wells plate labelled PCR plate . Add 3 µL RNA to each well.
Seal, vortex ( 1600 rpm, °C, 00:01:00 ), centrifuge ( 500 x g, °C, for 00:01:00 ).

Place on a thermal cycler and run the Flu RT-PCR program as described below. Preheat Lid: 105 °C and Reaction Volume: 15 µL Temperature (°C) Time Cycle 1 42 60 minutes 2 94 2 minutes 3 94 20 seconds 5 cycles 4 44 30 seconds 5 68 3 minutes 6 94 20 seconds 35 cycles 7 58 30 seconds 8 68 3 minutes 9 68 5 minutes 10 4 Hold Flu RT-PCR program Safe Stopping Point – seal the plate and store at 2°C to 8°C or leave on the thermal cycler overnight.
Post tagmentation Clean Up step Reagent Storage Instructions 1 ST2 (Stop Tagment Buffer 2) Room Temp Vortex before use. 2 TWB (Tagmentation Wash Buffer) 2°C to 8°C Invert to mix. Overview of reagents

Prepare Tagmentation Master Mix, add cDNA and run Tagmentation as described in the steps below. Reagent Storage Instructions 1 EBLT (Enrichment BLT) 2°C to 8°C Bring to room temperature. Vortex thoroughly before use. 2 TB1 (Tagmentation Buffer 1) -25°C to -15°C Bring to room temperature. Vortex thoroughly before use. Overview of reagents

Prepare the TAG PCR Master Mix and perform TAG PCR following the steps below. Reagent Storage Instructions 1 EPM (Enriched PCR Mix) -25°C to -15°C Invert to mix. Keep on ice until use. 2 Index Adapters -25°C to -15°C Thaw at room temperature. Vortex to mix and then centrifuge. Keep on ice. Overview of reagents

Reagent Storage Instructions 1 IPB (Illumina Purification Beads) Room Temperature Vortex thoroughly to mix. 2 RSB (Resuspension Buffer) 2°C to 8°C Bring to room temperature. Invert to mix. 3 Ethanol Room Temperature Prepare a fresh solution of 80% EtOH Overview of reagents
Quantify and normalise libraries following the steps below:
PCR Clean Up Step
Centrifuge the PCR plate at 500 x g, °C, for 00:01:00 .
Vortex to resuspend the IPB and add 9 µL to each well of the PCR plate.
Vortex to mix and centrifuge briefly.
Incubate at Room temperature for 00:05:00
Place it on a magnetic stand and wait for the liquid to clear (~ 00:05:00 ). Remove and discard the supernatant.
Wash beads as follows:a.Keep the plate on the magnetic stand and add 100 µL 80% EtOH to each well.b.Wait 00:00:30 , then remove and discard the supernatant.
Wash the beads a second time.
Centrifuge the plate briefly to collect any remaining EtOH at the bottom. Place it on the magnetic stand and wait for the liquid to clear. Use a P20 pipette to remove ALL residual EtOH.
Allow to air-dry on the magnetic stand for 00:01:00
Add 16 µL RSB to resuspend the beads. Vortex to mix and then centrifuge briefly.
Incubate at Room temperature for 00:02:00 .
Place the plate on the magnetic rack and wait for the liquid to clear (~ 00:03:00 ).
Transfer 15 µL of supernatant to the wells of a new PCR plate labelled cDNA .
At least 10% of the samples should be checked post-PCR using a Qubit Fluorometer and Qubit DNA HS Assay to ensure amplification was successful.

In a microcentrifuge tube, combine the following reagents to prepare the Tagmentation Master Mix. The table indicates the required volume for 1 sample. Calculate the volumes required for the amount of samples included in the run. *volumes include overage. Reagent Volume (μL) per sample Required volume for x samples 1 EBLT 2 ... 2 TB1 6 ... 3 Nuclease-free water 10 ... 4 Total 18 ... Tagmentation Master Mix
Add 15 µL of the master mix to each well of a new PCR plate labelled TAG . Add 10 µL from the cDNA plate to the corresponding wells of the TAG plate .
Seal, vortex 1600 rpm, °C, 1 minute , centrifuge 500 x g, °C, 1 minute .
Place on a thermal cycler and run the TAG program. Preheat Lid: 105 °C and Reaction Volume: 25 µL Temperature (°C) Time 1 55 5 minutes 2 10 Hold TAG Program
After the TAG program, immediately add 5 µL ST2 to each well of the TAG plate.
Seal, vortex 1600 rpm, °C, 1 minute , centrifuge 500 x g, °C, 1 minute .
Incubate 00:05:00 at Room temperature .
Centrifuge briefly.
Place it on a magnetic stand and wait until the liquid is clear (approximately 00:03:00 ) Remove and discard the supernatant.

Wash beads as follows: a. Remove the plate from the magnetic stand. b. Add 50 µL TWB to each well. c. Seal, vortex 1600 rpm, °C, 1 minute , centrifuge ( 500 x g, °C, 1 minute . d. Place it on the magnetic stand and wait until the liquid is clear (~ 00:02:00 ). e. Remove and discard the supernatant.
Wash the beads a second time. Leave supernatant in the plate after the second wash to prevent beads from overdrying.

In a microcentrifuge tube, combine the following reagents to prepare the TAG PCR Master Mix. *volumes include overage. Reagent Volume (μL) per sample Required volume for x samples 1 EPM 12 ... 2 Nuclease-free water 12 ... 3 Total 24 ... TAG PCR Master Mix
Vortex the TAG PCR master mix thoroughly.
Keep the TAG plate on the magnetic stand and remove the TWB . Use a P20 pipette to remove any remaining TWB from each well.
Remove the TAG plate from the magnetic stand.
Add 20 µL TAG PCR master mix to each well. Add 5 µL Index Adapters to each well.
Seal the plate, vortex at 1600 rpm, °C, 1 minute , and centrifuge at 500 x g, °C, 1 minute .

Place the plate on a thermal cycler and run the TAG PCR program. Preheat Lid: 100 °C and Reaction Volume: 25 µL Temperature (°C) Time Cycle 1 72 3 minutes 2 98 3 minutes 3 98 20 seconds 7 cycles* 4 60 30 seconds 5 72 1 minutes 6 72 3 minutes 7 10 Hold � TAG PCR Program *To compensate for lower cDNA input amounts, the number of PCR cycles can be increased as follows: Total cDNA Input (ng) Number of PCR cycles 1 1-24 12 2 25-99 9 3 100-500 7
Safe Stopping Point - seal the plate and store at -25°C to -15°C
Allow the plate to air-dry on the magnetic stand for 00:01:00 .
Add 16 µL RSB to each well to resuspend the beads.
Vortex to mix and centrifuge briefly.
Transfer 15 µL of the supernatant to a new final PCR plate. Safe Stopping Point - seal the plate and store at -25°C to -15°C
Incubate at Room temperature for 00:02:00 .
Place the plate on the magnetic rack and wait for the liquid to clear (~ 00:03:00 ).
Label a new PCR plate IPB .
Place it on a magnetic stand and wait for the liquid to clear (~ 00:05:00 ). Without disturbing the beads, remove and discard the supernatant.
Wash the beads as follows: a. With the plate on the magnetic stand, add 100 µL of 80% EtOH to each well. b. Incubate for 00:00:30 , then remove and discard the supernatant.
Wash the beads a second time.

After the second wash, briefly centrifuge the plate to collect any remaining EtOH at the bottom of the wells. Place it back on the magnetic stand and wait for the liquid to clear. Using a P20 pipette, remove and discard ALL residual EtOH from the wells.
Vortex to mix and centrifuge briefly.
Incubate at Room temperature for 00:05:00 .
Transfer 25 µL supernatant from each well of the TAG plate to the corresponding wells of the IPB plate.
Vortex the IPB tube to resuspend and add 22.5 µL IPB to each well of the IPB plate.
Centrifuge the TAG plate at 500 x g, °C, for 00:01:00 .
Place it on a magnetic stand and wait until the liquid is clear (~ 00:03:00 ).
Analyse 1 µL of each prepared library using the Qubit dsDNA HS Assay on a Qubit fluorometer.
Calculate the molarity of each prepared library ( use 350 bp as the average library size) .
Dilute each library to a normalized concentration of 4 nanomolar (nM) using RSB .
Pool 5 µL from each library into a clean 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube.
Quantify the pool by qPCR using the Takara Library Quantification Kit .
[Optional] Run 1 µL of the library pool on a TapeStation using the D1000 ScreenTape assay to confirm the average library size.
Using the qPCR quantification, dilute the library to the starting concentration for your chosen sequencing platform/kit.
Follow the denature and dilute instructions for your chosen sequencing platform by referring to the manufacturer's user guides.

Sequencing Platform/Kit Starting Concentration (nM) Final Loading Concentration (pM) 1 NextSeq (Standard-SBS) 2 750 2 NextSeq (XLEAP-SBS) 2 650 3 MiSeq i100 2 80 4 MiSeq V2 4 10-12 5 MiSeq V3 4 12-14 Loading Concentration Recommendations by Platform/ Kit
